judgments.comparative_verdict: Report 2 answered better overall — it correctly flags F-06 as a product/sign-off judgment call — though it omits the local-setup/RESULTS_DB collision question (d) that both reports fail to fully address.

report_1.evidence: Team A — Registry config (F-02, F-05, F-09): 3 simple, independent changes to pipeline/registry.py

report_2.evidence: Group A (1 engineer): F-02, F-05, F-09 - All in pipeline/registry.py ... Rationale: Single file, three simple constants; one engineer avoids merge conflicts

report_1.evidence: Recommended execution order: 1. Start in parallel: Teams A, B, C (simplest, zero dependencies) 2. Then: Team D (Rubric fix; enables F-11 test) 3. Finally: Team E (Batch runner; most complex and interdependent)

report_2.evidence: Wave 1 — Configuration & Documentation (fully parallelizable, no code dependencies) Group A (1 engineer): F-02, F-05, F-09 ... Wave 2 — Code fixes (parallelizable across file boundaries ... Group F (1 engineer): F-04, F-07, F-13, F-14 (treated as a single logical unit

F-06: Requires product/domain decision before implementation ... Changing scoring thresholds is a product decision, not a bug fix. It affects the output of every score in the pipeline ... Escalate F-06 to a decision gate; don't assign until answered.
